title: [WIP] Add conmon-rs to testing matrix
body:
***Status***: This PR is mostly "on hold", with multiple failures due to need for additional dependent work in most codepaths and tests.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign saschagrunert for approval by writing `/assign @saschagrunert` in a comment. For more information see:[The Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
![cirrus_yml](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1183438/182707690-7ee27d42-01e6-48c9-a527-41572fa9e1f7.png)
This PR is on hold until some conmon-rs package and/or binary availability issues are worked out.
Ref: https://github.com/containers/conmon-rs/pull/602

@mheon or @baude do you have any suggestions of how we (or someone) should deal with [the plethora of version-format warnings](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6222574144192512/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host-rs.log.html)?  Surely they'll drive users (and probably the system-tests) crazy.
Opened https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15335 and assigned to Matt as requested.
force-push: Rebased onto `main` ***AND*** #15340
Force-push: Rebased on main
Force-push: Rebased on main
Force-push: Rebased on main

I'm not sure why my review is requested; it doesn't look like there's any point to this until conmon-rs learns to grok the `--api-version` option...?
```
error: Found argument '--api-version' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context
```
I think `--api-version` is left over from the conmon args. It's not applicable anymore

@edsantiago thanks for looking, no this is nowhere near ready for even an initial review.  I'm not sure why Jhon poked you, it's my understanding that a lot of work is needed in `conmon-rs` and our tests before this can move forward.  I just keep rebasing it weekly so it doesn't bit-rot too badly.

> I think --api-version is left over from the conmon args. It's not applicable anymore

Ooh, should it just be clobbered from the tests then?  I can maybe figure out how to do that.
Note to me: Investigate `int podman root container` - task should not exist, some matrix is leaking.
I'm going to leave this open, but basically ignore it for now.  Just in case some maintainer wants to force-push to my branch to trigger a CI run.

@cevich: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Hey, I'm getting nervous about this PR being stalled so long. Could some conmon expert please look at this: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/13a1c55d3f0d0cb9a34e865844278e935337cd1b/libpod/oci_conmon_common.go#L1291-L1301

...and come up with the right magic `if` statement to check for `env("CONMON_BINARY") has "rs"`, then define the proper set of args?
But we don't have conmon-rs support fully written yet, so even if we add tests they will not pass?

My thinking was that maybe fewer tests might fail, since right now every single test fails with "conmonrs does not grok --api-version 1". Finding the right set of options for calling conmonrs might reduce the errors, and perhaps even reveal some interesting ones. (But, I don't know anything at all about the state of podman+conmonrs, so maybe it would still be 100% failure. So, feel free to ignore.)
I wouldn't say 100% failure, but I would expect ~75% failure from things like attach not working

...and running it rootless and inside a container and remote and...

Maybe we should just close this PR, and re-open it later when conmon-rs development is more "nailed-down"?
Probably a good idea
